chief_complaint: A 26-year-old male patient presented with dry mouth, polydipsia, and polyuria lasting for 2 months.
history: The patient was admitted with symptoms of dry mouth, polydipsia, and polyuria lasting for 2 months. He was diagnosed with maturity-onset diabetes of the young type 6 (MODY6) based on blood glucose levels, glycosylated hemoglobin results, familial co-segregation of the variant, and genetic sequencing data. The patient had a heterozygous neurogenic differentiation factor 1 (NEUROD1) c.-108G>C mutation.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Blood glucose levels, glycosylated hemoglobin results, genetic sequencing data, and familial co-segregation of the variant were performed. The results showed a heterozygous NEUROD1 c.-108G>C mutation.
correct_diagnosis: Maturity-onset diabetes of the young type 6 (MODY6) with a heterozygous NEUROD1 c.-108G>C mutation
differentials: Maturity-onset diabetes of the young type 6 (MODY6) with a heterozygous NEUROD1 c.-108G>C mutation
correct_ranking: Maturity-onset diabetes of the young type 6 (MODY6) with a heterozygous NEUROD1 c.-108G>C mutation
